2.description: Equilibrium bids and expected payoffs of bidders. For pure private valuesα=1$\protect \alpha =1$and pure common valuesα=1/2$\protect \alpha =1/2$, and for valuesv(s)=s$v(s)=s$, Panels A and B plot equilibrium bids as functions of signals$s$for the initiating bidder (solid blue line) and the noninitiating bidder (dashed red line) in a bidder-initiated auction, and either of the two bidders (dash-dotted pink line) in a seller-initiated auction. Panels C and D plot the corresponding expected surpluses. The cutoff signal for initiation,ŝ$\hat{s}$, is 0.5. The lowest and highest serious bids are indicated bya̲(ŝ)$\protect \underline{a}(\hat{s})$anda¯(ŝ)$\bar{a}(\hat{s})$. For signals of the noninitiating bidder below the lowest serious bida̲(ŝ)$\protect \underline{a}(\hat{s})$, Panel A plots one possible equilibrium bid. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: Equilibria with seller- and bidder-initiated auctions, pure private values. For pure private valuesα=1$\protect \alpha =1$, valuesv(s)=s$v(s)=s$, no exogenous liquidity shocksν=0$\protect \nu =0$, and parametersβ=0.9$\protect \beta =0.9$andλ=0.9$\protect \lambda =0.9$, the figure plots period-0 best responses (BR) of the seller, who chooses the probability of putting the asset up for sale without receiving any indications of interestμ$\protect \mu$(solid black line), and each bidder, who chooses the cutoff signal for initiationŝ$\hat{s}$(dashed red line), as well as multiple pure and mixed equilibria (circle markers). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
